briefs.title: BC Expands Drug Coverage and Bans Extra Charges to Patients Starting March 1, 2026
briefs.summary: Effective March 1, 2026, BC is adding a new drug plan called Plan NP for all BC medicare cardholders, removing a previous exemption that excluded insulin from price controls, and banning pharmacies and other drug providers from charging patients any out-of-pocket fees for drugs fully covered under a provincial drug plan (except nutritional vitamins or supplements for people with cystic fibrosis).
briefs.action_required: Stop charging patients any fees for drugs fully covered by a BC drug plan by March 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Drug Plan changes (Plan NP, insulin pricing, no patient charges) take effect
